tags.name: norm:fcvt-bf16-s_op
tags.text: Narrowing convert FP32 value to a BF16 value. Round according to the RM field.

tags.name: norm:fcvt-s-bf16_op
tags.text: Converts a BF16 value to an FP32 value. The conversion is exact.

tags.name: norm:vfncvtbf16-f-f-w_op
tags.text: Narrowing convert from FP32 to BF16. Round according to the frm register.

tags.name: norm:vfncvtbf16-f-f-w_sew_rsv
tags.text: SEW is any value other than 16

tags.name: norm:vfwcvtbf16-f-f-v_op
tags.text: Widening convert from BF16 to FP32. The conversion is exact.

tags.name: norm:vfwcvtbf16-f-f-v_sew_rsv
tags.text: SEW is any value other than 16

tags.name: norm:vfwmaccbf16_op
tags.text: This instruction performs a widening fused multiply-accumulate operation, where each pair of BF16 values are multiplied and their unrounded product is added to the corresponding FP32 accumulate value. The sum is rounded according to the frm register.

tags.name: norm:vfwmaccbf16_vv
tags.text: In the vector-vector version, the BF16 elements are read from vs1 and vs2 and FP32 accumulate value is read from vd. The FP32 result is written to the destination register vd.

tags.name: norm:bf16_subnorm
tags.text: All of the BF16 instructions in the extensions defined in this specification (i.e., Zfbfmin, Zvfbfmin, and Zvfbfwma) fully support subnormal numbers. That is, instructions are able to accept subnormal values as inputs and they can produce subnormal results.

tags.name: norm:vfwmaccbf16_op
tags.text: This instruction performs a widening fused multiply-accumulate operation, where each pair of BF16 values are multiplied and their unrounded product is added to the corresponding FP32 accumulate value. The sum is rounded according to the frm register.

tags.name: norm:vfwmaccbf16_vf
tags.text: The vector-scalar version is similar, but instead of reading elements from vs1, a scalar BF16 value is read from the FPU register rs1.

tags.name: norm:bf16_subnorm
tags.text: All of the BF16 instructions in the extensions defined in this specification (i.e., Zfbfmin, Zvfbfmin, and Zvfbfwma) fully support subnormal numbers. That is, instructions are able to accept subnormal values as inputs and they can produce subnormal results.

tags.name: norm:vfwmaccbf16_sew_rsv
tags.text: SEW is any value other than 16

tags.name: norm:vfwmaccbf16_sew_rsv
tags.text: SEW is any value other than 16

normative_rules.name: Zca_offsets_mul2
normative_rules.summary: compressed instruction offsets are multiples of 2

tags.name: norm:Zca_offsets_mul2
tags.text:
As with base RVI instructions, the offsets of all RVC
control transfer instructions are in multiples of 2 bytes.

tags.name: norm:Zca_align16
tags.text:
The C extension allows 16-bit instructions to be freely
intermixed with 32-bit instructions, with the latter now able to start
on any 16-bit boundary, i.e., IALIGN=16.

tags.name: norm:Zca_no_misaligned
tags.text:
With the addition of the C
extension, no instructions can raise instruction-address-misaligned
exceptions.

tags.name: norm:Zca_regs8
tags.text:
CIW,
CL, CS, CA, and CB are limited to just 8 of them.
<<registers>> lists these popular registers, which
correspond to registers x8 to x15.

tags.name: norm:Zc_fp_regs
tags.text:
Compressed register-based floating-point loads and stores also use the
CL and CS formats respectively, with the eight registers mapping to f8 to f15.

tags.name: norm:c-lwsp_op
tags.text:
C.LWSP loads a 32-bit value from memory into register rd. It computes
an effective address by adding the zero-extended offset, scaled by 4,
to the stack pointer, x2. It expands to lw rd, offset(x2).

tags.name: norm:c-lwsp_rsv
tags.text:
C.LWSP is
valid only when rd≠x0; the code points with rd=x0 are reserved.

tags.name: norm:c-ldsp_op
tags.text:
C.LDSP is an RV64C-only instruction that loads a 64-bit value
from memory into register rd. It computes its effective address by
adding the zero-extended offset, scaled by 8, to the stack pointer,
x2. It expands to ld rd, offset(x2).

tags.name: norm:c-ldsp_rsv
tags.text:
C.LDSP is valid only when
rd≠x0; the code points with
rd=x0 are reserved.

tags.name: norm:c-flwsp_op
tags.text:
C.FLWSP is an RV32FC-only instruction that loads a single-precision
floating-point value from memory into floating-point register rd. It
computes its effective address by adding the zero-extended offset,
scaled by 4, to the stack pointer, x2. It expands to
flw rd, offset(x2).

tags.name: norm:c-fdwsp_op
tags.text:
C.FLDSP is an RV32DC/RV64DC-only instruction that loads a
double-precision floating-point value from memory into floating-point
register rd. It computes its effective address by adding the
zero-extended offset, scaled by 8, to the stack pointer, x2. It
expands to fld rd, offset(x2).

tags.name: norm:c-swsp_op
tags.text:
C.SWSP stores a 32-bit value in register rs2 to memory. It computes an
effective address by adding the zero-extended offset, scaled by 4, to
the stack pointer, x2. It expands to sw rs2, offset(x2).

tags.name: norm:c-sdsp_op
tags.text:
C.SDSP is an RV64C-only instruction that stores a 64-bit value in
register rs2 to memory. It computes an effective address by adding the
zero-extended offset, scaled by 8, to the stack pointer, x2. It
expands to sd rs2, offset(x2).

tags.name: norm:c-fswsp_op
tags.text:
C.FSWSP is an RV32FC-only instruction that stores a single-precision
floating-point value in floating-point register rs2 to memory. It
computes an effective address by adding the zero-extended offset,
scaled by 4, to the stack pointer, x2. It expands to
fsw rs2, offset(x2).

tags.name: norm:c-fsdwsp_op
tags.text:
C.FSDSP is an RV32DC/RV64DC-only instruction that stores a
double-precision floating-point value in floating-point register rs2
to memory. It computes an effective address by adding the
zero-extended offset, scaled by 8, to the stack pointer, x2. It
expands to fsd rs2, offset(x2).

tags.name: norm:c-lw_op
tags.text:
C.LW loads a 32-bit value from memory into register
rd′. It computes an effective address by adding the
zero-extended offset, scaled by 4, to the base address in register
rs1′. It expands to lw rd′, offset(rs1′).

tags.name: norm:c-ld_op
tags.text:
C.LD is an RV64C-only instruction that loads a 64-bit value from
memory into register rd′. It computes an effective
address by adding the zero-extended offset, scaled by 8, to the base
address in register rs1′. It expands to
ld rd′, offset(rs1′).

tags.name: norm:c-flw_op
tags.text:
C.FLW is an RV32FC-only instruction that loads a single-precision
floating-point value from memory into floating-point register
rd′. It computes an effective address by adding the
zero-extended offset, scaled by 4, to the base address in register
rs1′. It expands to
flw rd′, offset(rs1′).

tags.name: norm:c-fld_op
tags.text:
C.FLD is an RV32DC/RV64DC-only instruction that loads a double-precision
floating-point value from memory into floating-point register
rd′. It computes an effective address by adding the
zero-extended offset, scaled by 8, to the base address in register
rs1′. It expands to
fld rd′, offset(rs1′).

tags.name: norm:c-sw_op
tags.text:
C.SW stores a 32-bit value in register rs2′ to memory.
It computes an effective address by adding the zero-extended offset,
scaled by 4, to the base address in register rs1′. It
expands to sw rs2′, offset(rs1′).

tags.name: norm:c-sd_op
tags.text:
C.SD is an RV64C-only instruction that stores a 64-bit value in
register rs2′ to memory. It computes an effective
address by adding the zero-extended offset, scaled by 8, to the base
address in register rs1′. It expands to
sd rs2′, offset(rs1′).

tags.name: norm:c-fsw_op
tags.text:
C.FSW is an RV32FC-only instruction that stores a single-precision
floating-point value in floating-point register rs2′ to
memory. It computes an effective address by adding the zero-extended
offset, scaled by 4, to the base address in register
rs1′. It expands to
fsw rs2′, offset(rs1′).

tags.name: norm:c-fsd_op
tags.text:
C.FSD is an RV32DC/RV64DC-only instruction that stores a
double-precision floating-point value in floating-point register
rs2′ to memory. It computes an effective address by
adding the zero-extended offset, scaled by 8, to the base address in
register rs1′. It expands to
fsd rs2′, offset(rs1′).

tags.name: norm:c-j_op
tags.text:
C.J performs an unconditional control transfer. The offset is
sign-extended and added to the pc to form the jump target address. C.J
can therefore target a &#177;2 KiB range. C.J expands to
jal x0, offset.

tags.name: norm:c-jal_op
tags.text:
C.JAL is an RV32C-only instruction that performs the same operation as
C.J, but additionally writes the address of the instruction following
the jump (pc+2) to the link register, x1. C.JAL expands to
jal x1, offset.

tags.name: norm:c-jr_op
tags.text:
C.JR (jump register) performs an unconditional control transfer to the
address in register rs1. C.JR expands to jalr x0, 0(rs1).

tags.name: norm:c-jr_rsv
tags.text:
C.JR is
valid only when rs1≠x0; the code
point with rs1=x0 is reserved.

tags.name: norm:c-jalr_op
tags.text:
C.JALR (jump and link register) performs the same operation as C.JR, but
additionally writes the address of the instruction following the jump
(pc+2) to the link register, x1. C.JALR expands to
jalr x1, 0(rs1).

tags.name: norm:c-jalr_ebreak
tags.text:
C.JALR is valid only when
rs1≠x0; the code point with
rs1=x0 corresponds to the C.EBREAK
instruction.

tags.name: norm:c-jalr_ebreak
tags.text:
C.JALR is valid only when
rs1≠x0; the code point with
rs1=x0 corresponds to the C.EBREAK
instruction.

tags.name: norm:c-beqz_op
tags.text:
C.BEQZ performs conditional control transfers. The offset is
sign-extended and added to the pc to form the branch target address.
It can therefore target a &#177;256 B range. C.BEQZ takes the
branch if the value in register rs1′ is zero. It
expands to beq rs1′, x0, offset.

tags.name: norm:c-bnez_op
tags.text:
C.BNEZ is defined analogously, but it takes the branch if
rs1′ contains a nonzero value. It expands to
bne rs1′, x0, offset.

tags.name: norm:c-li_op
tags.text:
C.LI loads the sign-extended 6-bit immediate, imm, into register rd.
C.LI expands into addi rd, x0, imm.

tags.name: norm:c-li_hint
tags.text: The C.LI code points with rd=x0 are HINTs.

tags.name: norm:c-lui_op
tags.text:
C.LUI loads the non-zero 6-bit immediate field into bits 17–12 of the
destination register, clears the bottom 12 bits, and sign-extends bit 17
into all higher bits of the destination. C.LUI expands into
lui rd, imm.

tags.name: norm:c-lui_rsv
tags.text:
C.LUI is valid only when
rd≠x2,
and when the immediate is not equal to zero. The code points with
imm=0 are reserved.
The code points with rd=x2 and imm≠0 correspond to the
C.ADDI16SP instruction.
The code points with rd=x0 and imm≠0 are HINTs.

tags.name: norm:c-addi_op
tags.text:
C.ADDI adds the non-zero sign-extended 6-bit immediate to the value in
register rd then writes the result to rd. C.ADDI expands into
addi rd, rd, imm.

tags.name: norm:c-addi_hint
tags.text: The code points with rd≠0 and imm=0 are HINTs.

tags.name: norm:c-addi_nop
tags.text:
The code points with rd=x0 encode the C.NOP instruction, of
which the code points with imm≠0 are HINTs.

tags.name: norm:c-addiw_op
tags.text:
C.ADDIW is an RV64C-only instruction that performs the same
computation but produces a 32-bit result, then sign-extends result to 64
bits. C.ADDIW expands into addiw rd, rd, imm. The immediate can be
zero for C.ADDIW, where this corresponds to sext.w rd.

tags.name: norm:c-addiw_rsv
tags.text:
C.ADDIW is
valid only when rd≠x0; the code points with
rd=x0 are reserved.

tags.name: norm:c-addi16sp_op
tags.text:
C.ADDI16SP (add immediate to stack pointer)
shares the opcode with C.LUI, but has a destination field of
x2. C.ADDI16SP adds the non-zero sign-extended 6-bit immediate to the
value in the stack pointer (sp=x2), where the immediate is scaled to
represent multiples of 16 in the range [-512, 496]. C.ADDI16SP is used to
adjust the stack pointer in procedure prologues and epilogues. It
expands into addi x2, x2, nzimm[9:4].

tags.name: norm:c-addi16sp_rsv
tags.text:
C.ADDI16SP is valid only when
nzimm≠0; the code point with nzimm=0 is reserved.

tags.name: norm:c-addi4spn_op
tags.text:
C.ADDI4SPN (add immediate to stack pointer, non-destructive)
is a CIW-format instruction that adds a zero-extended
non-zero immediate, scaled by 4, to the stack pointer, x2, and writes
the result to rd′. This instruction is used to generate
pointers to stack-allocated variables, and expands to
addi rd′, x2, nzuimm[9:2].

tags.name: norm:c-addi4spn_rsv
tags.text:
C.ADDI4SPN is valid only when
nzuimm≠0; the code points with nzuimm=0 are
reserved.

tags.name: norm:c-slli_op
tags.text:
C.SLLI is a CI-format instruction that performs a logical left shift of
the value in register rd then writes the result to rd. The shift
amount is encoded in the shamt field.
C.SLLI expands into slli rd, rd, shamt[5:0].

tags.name: norm:c-slli_hint
tags.text: The C.SLLI code points with shamt=0 or with rd=x0 are HINTs.

tags.name: norm:c-slli_shamt5
tags.text:
For RV32C, shamt[5] must be zero; the code points with shamt[5]=1
are designated for custom extensions.

tags.name: norm:c-srli_op
tags.text:
C.SRLI is a CB-format instruction that performs a logical right shift of
the value in register rd′ then writes the result to
rd′. The shift amount is encoded in the shamt field.
C.SRLI expands into srli rd′, rd′, shamt.

tags.name: norm:c-srli_hint
tags.text: The C.SRLI code points with shamt=0 are HINTs.

tags.name: norm:c-srli_shamt5
tags.text:
For RV32C, shamt[5] must be zero; the code points with shamt[5]=1
are designated for custom extensions.

tags.name: norm:c-srai_op
tags.text:
C.SRAI is defined analogously to C.SRLI, but instead performs an
arithmetic right shift. C.SRAI expands to
srai rd′, rd′, shamt.

tags.name: norm:c-andi_op
tags.text:
C.ANDI is a CB-format instruction that computes the bitwise AND of the
value in register rd′ and the sign-extended 6-bit
immediate, then writes the result to rd′. C.ANDI
expands to andi rd′, rd′, imm.

tags.name: norm:c-mv_op
tags.text:
C.MV copies the value in register rs2 into register rd. C.MV expands
into add rd, x0, rs2.

tags.name: norm:c-mv_jr
tags.text:
C.MV is valid only when
rs2≠x0; the code points with rs2=x0 correspond to the C.JR instruction.

tags.name: norm:c-mv_hint
tags.text: The code points with rs2≠x0 and rd=x0 are HINTs.

tags.name: norm:c-add_op
tags.text:
C.ADD adds the values in registers rd and rs2 and writes the result
to register rd. C.ADD expands into add rd, rd, rs2.

tags.name: norm:c-add_val
tags.text:
C.ADD is only
valid when rs2≠x0; the code points with rs2=x0 correspond to the C.JALR
and C.EBREAK instructions.

tags.name: norm:c-add_hint
tags.text: The code points with rs2≠x0 and rd=x0 are HINTs.

tags.name: norm:c-and_op
tags.text:
C.AND computes the bitwise AND of the values in registers
rd′ and rs2′, then writes the result
to register rd′. C.AND expands into
and rd′, rd′, rs2′.

tags.name: norm:c-or_op
tags.text:
C.OR computes the bitwise OR of the values in registers
rd′ and rs2′, then writes the result
to register rd′. C.OR expands into
or rd′, rd′, rs2′.

tags.name: norm:c-xor_op
tags.text:
C.XOR computes the bitwise XOR of the values in registers
rd′ and rs2′, then writes the result
to register rd′. C.XOR expands into
xor rd′, rd′, rs2′.

tags.name: norm:c-sub_op
tags.text:
C.SUB subtracts the value in register rs2′ from the
value in register rd′, then writes the result to
register rd′. C.SUB expands into
sub rd′, rd′, rs2′.

tags.name: norm:c-addw_op
tags.text:
C.ADDW is an RV64C-only instruction that adds the values in
registers rd′ and rs2′, then
sign-extends the lower 32 bits of the sum before writing the result to
register rd′. C.ADDW expands into
addw rd′, rd′, rs2′.

tags.name: norm:c-subw_op
tags.text:
C.SUBW is an RV64C-only instruction that subtracts the value in
register rs2′ from the value in register
rd′, then sign-extends the lower 32 bits of the
difference before writing the result to register rd′.
C.SUBW expands into subw rd′, rd′, rs2′.

tags.name: norm:Zca_illegal
tags.text:
A 16-bit instruction with all bits zero is permanently reserved as an
illegal instruction.

tags.name: norm:c-nop_op
tags.text:
C.NOP is a CI-format instruction that does not change any user-visible
state, except for advancing the pc and incrementing any applicable
performance counters. C.NOP expands to nop.

tags.name: norm:c-nop_hint
tags.text:
The C.NOP code points
with imm≠0 encode HINTs.

tags.name: norm:c-ebreak_op
tags.text:
Debuggers can use the C.EBREAK instruction, which expands to ebreak,
to cause control to be transferred back to the debugging environment.
C.EBREAK shares the opcode with the C.ADD instruction, but with rd and
rs2 both zero, thus can also use the CR format.

tags.name: norm:Zca_hints
tags.text:
A portion of the RVC encoding space is reserved for microarchitectural
HINTs. Like the HINTs in the RV32I base ISA (see
<<rv32i-hints>>), these instructions do not
modify any architectural state, except for advancing the pc and any
applicable performance counters. HINTs are executed as no-ops on
implementations that ignore them.

normative_rules.name: PPO_overlap
normative_rules.summary: PPO overlapping address rule for CMO instructions

tags.name: norm:PPO_overlap_1
tags.text:
if a precedes b in program order,
then a will precede b in the global memory order if:

tags.name: norm:PPO_overlap_2
tags.text:
a is an invalidate, clean, or flush, b is a load, and a and b access
overlapping memory addresses

normative_rules.name: cmo_invalidate_load_values
normative_rules.summary: Load value rules for invalidate operations

tags.name: norm:invalidate_load_val1
tags.text:
If no clean or flush operations on x precede i in the global memory order,
either the initial value of x or the value of any store to x that precedes
i

tags.name: norm:invalidate_load_val2
tags.text:
If no store to x precedes a clean or flush operation on x in the global
memory order and if the clean or flush operation on x precedes i in the
global memory order, either the initial value of x or the value of any store
to x that precedes i

tags.name: norm:invalidate_load_val3
tags.text:
If a store to x precedes a clean or flush operation on x in the global
memory order and if the clean or flush operation on x precedes i in the
global memory order, either the value of the latest store to x that precedes
the latest clean or flush operation on x or the value of any store to x
that both precedes i and succeeds the latest clean or flush operation on x
that precedes i

tags.name: norm:invalidate_load_val4
tags.text:
The value of any store to x by a non-coherent agent regardless of the above
conditions

normative_rules.name: cbo_physical_address_constraints
normative_rules.summary: Physical address constraints for CBO instructions in cache blocks

tags.name: norm:PMP_same
tags.text:
The PMP access control bits shall be the same for all physical addresses in
the cache block, and if write permission is granted by the PMP access control
bits, read permission shall also be granted

tags.name: norm:PMA_same
tags.text:
The PMAs shall be the same for all physical addresses in the cache block,
and if write permission is granted by the supported access type PMAs, read
permission shall also be granted

tags.name: norm:cbo_rsv
tags.text:
If the above constraints are not met, the behavior of a CBO instruction is
UNSPECIFIED.

normative_rules.name: cbm_access
normative_rules.summary: Cache-block management instruction access permission

tags.name: norm:cbm_access
tags.text:
A cache-block management instruction is permitted to access the specified cache
block whenever a load instruction or store instruction is permitted to access
the corresponding physical addresses.

normative_rules.name: cbm_unperm_fault
normative_rules.summary: Cache-block management instruction fault on unpermitted access

tags.name: norm:cbm_unperm_fault
tags.text:
If
access to the cache block is not permitted, a cache-block management instruction
raises a store page-fault or store guest-page-fault exception if address
translation does not permit any access or raises a store access-fault exception
otherwise.

normative_rules.name: cbm_unperm_translate
normative_rules.summary: Cache-block management instruction address translation behavior

tags.name: norm:cbm_unperm_translate
tags.text:
During address translation, the instruction also checks the accessed
bit and may either raise an exception or set the bit as required.

normative_rules.name: cbz_access
normative_rules.summary: Cache-block zero instruction access permission

tags.name: norm:cbz_access
tags.text:
A cache-block zero instruction is permitted to access the specified cache block
whenever a store instruction is permitted to access the corresponding physical
addresses and when the PMAs indicate that cache-block zero instructions are a
supported access type.

normative_rules.name: cbz_unperm_fault
normative_rules.summary: Cache-block zero instruction fault on unpermitted access

tags.name: norm:cbz_unperm_fault
tags.text:
If access to the cache block is not permitted, a
cache-block zero instruction raises a store page-fault or store guest-page-fault
exception if address translation does not permit write access or raises a store
access-fault exception otherwise.

normative_rules.name: cbz_unperm_translate
normative_rules.summary: Cache-block zero instruction address translation behavior

tags.name: norm:cbz_unperm_translate
tags.text:
During address translation, the instruction
also checks the accessed and dirty bits and may either raise an exception or set
the bits as required.

normative_rules.name: cbp_access
normative_rules.summary: Cache-block prefetch instruction access permission

tags.name: norm:cbp_access
tags.text:
A cache-block prefetch instruction is permitted to access the specified cache
block whenever a load instruction, store instruction, or instruction fetch is
permitted to access the corresponding physical addresses.

normative_rules.name: cbp_unperm_noexcep
normative_rules.summary: Cache-block prefetch instruction no exception on unpermitted access

tags.name: norm:cbp_unperm_noexcep
tags.text:
If access to the cache
block is not permitted, a cache-block prefetch instruction does not raise any
exceptions and shall not access any caches or memory.

normative_rules.name: cbp_unperm_translate
normative_rules.summary: Cache-block prefetch instruction address translation behavior

tags.name: norm:cbp_unperm_translate
tags.text:
During address
translation, the instruction does not check the accessed and dirty bits and
neither raises an exception nor sets the bits.

normative_rules.name: fault_excep_csr
normative_rules.summary: CMO instruction fault exception CSR behavior

tags.name: norm:fault_excep_csr
tags.text:
When a page-fault, guest-page-fault, or access-fault exception is taken, the
relevant *tval CSR is written with the faulting effective address (i.e. the
value of rs1).

normative_rules.name: no_addr_misaligned_excep
normative_rules.summary: CMO instructions do not generate address-misaligned exceptions

tags.name: norm:no_addr_misaligned_excep
tags.text: CMO instructions do not generate address-misaligned exceptions.

normative_rules.name: h_trans_cache
normative_rules.summary: CMO instruction hypervisor transformation

tags.name: norm:h_trans_cache
tags.text: For the purposes of writing the mtinst or htinst register on a trap, the following standard transformation is defined for cache-block management instructions and cache-block zero instructions:

normative_rules.name: henvcfg_present
normative_rules.summary: h{csrname} register presence requirement

tags.name: norm:henvcfg_present
tags.text:
The h{csrname} register is only present if the H-extension is
implemented and enabled.

normative_rules.name: xenvcfg-CBIE_field_behavior
normative_rules.summary: CBIE field behavior for CBO.INVAL instruction execution control

tags.name: norm:xenvcfg-CBIE_op
tags.text: Enables the execution of the cache block invalidate instruction, CBO.INVAL, in a lower privilege mode:

tags.name: norm:xenvcfg-CBIE_00
tags.text:
00: The instruction raises an illegal-instruction or virtual-instruction
exception

tags.name: norm:xenvcfg-CBIE_01
tags.text: 01: The instruction is executed and performs a flush operation

tags.name: norm:xenvcfg-CBIE_11
tags.text: 11: The instruction is executed and performs an invalidate operation

normative_rules.name: xenvcfg-CBCFE_field_behavior
normative_rules.summary: CBCFE field behavior for CBO.CLEAN and CBO.FLUSH instruction execution control

tags.name: norm:xenvcfg-CBCFE_op
tags.text: Enables the execution of the cache block clean instruction, CBO.CLEAN, and the cache block flush instruction, CBO.FLUSH, in a lower privilege mode:

tags.name: norm:xenvcfg-CBCFE_0
tags.text:
0: The instruction raises an illegal-instruction or virtual-instruction
exception

tags.name: norm:xenvcfg-CBCFE_1
tags.text: 1: The instruction is executed

normative_rules.name: xenvcfg-CBZE_field_behavior
normative_rules.summary: CBZE field behavior for CBO.ZERO instruction execution control

tags.name: norm:xenvcfg-CBZE_op
tags.text: Enables the execution of the cache block zero instruction, CBO.ZERO, in a lower privilege mode:

tags.name: norm:xenvcfg-CBZE_0
tags.text:
0: The instruction raises an illegal-instruction or virtual-instruction
exception

tags.name: norm:xenvcfg-CBZE_1
tags.text: 1: The instruction is executed

normative_rules.name: cbo-inval
normative_rules.summary: CBO.INVAL instruction execution control

tags.name: norm:cbo-inval
tags.text: A CBO.INVAL instruction executes or raises either an illegal-instruction exception or a virtual-instruction exception based on the state of the xenvcfg.CBIE fields:

normative_rules.name: cbo-clean_cbo-flush
normative_rules.summary: CBO.CLEAN and CBO.FLUSH instruction execution control

tags.name: norm:cbo-clean_cbo-flush
tags.text: A CBO.CLEAN or CBO.FLUSH instruction executes or raises an illegal-instruction or virtual-instruction exception based on the state of the xenvcfg.CBCFE bits:

normative_rules.name: cbo-zero_basedon_xenvcfg-CBZE
normative_rules.summary: CBO.ZERO execution control based on xenvcfg.CBZE

tags.name: norm:cbo-zero_basedon_xenvcfg-CBZE
tags.text: Finally, a CBO.ZERO instruction executes or raises an illegal-instruction or virtual-instruction exception based on the state of the xenvcfg.CBZE bits:

normative_rules.name: cbxe_unaffected
normative_rules.summary: CBIE/CBCFE/CBZE fields do not affect other registers

tags.name: norm:cbxe_unaffected
tags.text: The CBIE/CBCFE/CBZE fields in each xenvcfg register do not affect the read and write behavior of the same fields in the other xenvcfg registers.

normative_rules.name: cbo-zero_specified_block
normative_rules.summary: CBO.ZERO operates on specified cache block

tags.name: norm:cbo-zero_specified_block
tags.text:
These instructions operate on the cache block, or the memory locations
corresponding to the cache block, whose effective address is specified in rs1.

normative_rules.name: prefetch_operating_block
normative_rules.summary: Prefetch instructions operate on specified cache block

tags.name: norm:prefetch_operating_block
tags.text:
These instructions operate on the cache block whose effective address is the sum
of the base address specified in rs1 and the sign-extended offset encoded in
imm[11:0], where imm[4:0] shall equal 0b00000.

normative_rules.name: cbo-clean_op
normative_rules.summary: CBO.CLEAN instruction operation

tags.name: norm:cbo-clean_op
tags.text:
A cbo.clean instruction performs a clean operation on the cache block whose
effective address is the base address specified in rs1.

normative_rules.name: cbo-clean_offset
normative_rules.summary: CBO.CLEAN offset operand behavior

tags.name: norm:cbo-clean_offset
tags.text:
The offset operand may
be omitted; otherwise, any expression that computes the offset shall evaluate to
zero.

normative_rules.name: cbo-flush_op
normative_rules.summary: CBO.FLUSH instruction operation

tags.name: norm:cbo-flush_op
tags.text:
A cbo.flush instruction performs a flush operation on the cache block whose
that contains the address specified in rs1.

normative_rules.name: cbo-flush_unaligned
normative_rules.summary: CBO.FLUSH does not require cache block alignment

tags.name: norm:cbo-flush_unaligned
tags.text:
It is not required that rs1 is
aligned to the size of a cache block.

normative_rules.name: cbo-inval_op
normative_rules.summary: CBO.INVAL instruction operation

tags.name: norm:cbo-inval_op
tags.text:
A cbo.inval instruction performs an invalidate operation on the cache block
that contains the address specified in rs1.

normative_rules.name: cbo-inval_unaligned
normative_rules.summary: CBO.INVAL does not require cache block alignment

tags.name: norm:cbo-inval_unaligned
tags.text:
It is not required that rs1 is
aligned to the size of a cache block.

normative_rules.name: cbo-zero_op
normative_rules.summary: CBO.ZERO instruction operation

tags.name: norm:cbo-zero_op
tags.text:
A cbo.zero instruction performs stores of zeros to the full set of bytes
corresponding to the cache block that contains the address specified in rs1.

normative_rules.name: cbo-zero_unaligned
normative_rules.summary: CBO.ZERO does not require cache block alignment

tags.name: norm:cbo-zero_unaligned
tags.text: It is not required that rs1 is aligned to the size of a cache block.

normative_rules.name: cbo-zero_offset
normative_rules.summary: CBO.ZERO offset operand behavior

tags.name: norm:cbo-zero_offset
tags.text: The assembly offset operand may be omitted. If it isn’t then any expression that computes the offset shall evaluate to zero.

normative_rules.name: prefetch-i_op
normative_rules.summary: PREFETCH.I instruction operation

tags.name: norm:prefetch-i_op
tags.text: A prefetch.i instruction indicates to hardware that the cache block whose effective address is the sum of the base address specified in rs1 and the sign-extended offset encoded in imm[11:0], where imm[4:0] equals 0b00000, is likely to be accessed by an instruction fetch in the near future.

normative_rules.name: prefetch-r_op
normative_rules.summary: PREFETCH.R instruction operation

tags.name: norm:prefetch-r_op
tags.text: A prefetch.r instruction indicates to hardware that the cache block whose effective address is the sum of the base address specified in rs1 and the sign-extended offset encoded in imm[11:0], where imm[4:0] equals 0b00000, is likely to be accessed by a data read (i.e. load) in the near future.

normative_rules.name: prefetch-w_op
normative_rules.summary: PREFETCH.W instruction operation

tags.name: norm:prefetch-w_op
tags.text: A prefetch.w instruction indicates to hardware that the cache block whose effective address is the sum of the base address specified in rs1 and the sign-extended offset encoded in imm[11:0], where imm[4:0] equals 0b00000, is likely to be accessed by a data write (i.e. store) in the near future.

tags.name: norm:zihpm_op_sz_mode_acc
tags.text: RISC-V ISAs provide a set of up to thirty-two 64-bit performance counters and timers that are accessible via unprivileged XLEN-bit read-only CSR registers 0xC00–0xC1F (when XLEN=32, the upper 32 bits are accessed via CSR registers 0xC80–0xC9F). These counters are divided between the "Zicntr" and "Zihpm" extensions.

tags.name: norm:zicntr_rdcycle_op
tags.text:
The RDCYCLE pseudoinstruction reads the low XLEN bits of the cycle
CSR which holds a count of the number of clock cycles executed by the
processor core on which the hart is running from an arbitrary start time
in the past. RDCYCLEH is only present when XLEN=32 and reads bits 63-32
of the same cycle counter.

tags.name: norm:zicntr_rdtime_op
tags.text:
The RDTIME pseudoinstruction reads the low XLEN bits of the "time" CSR,
which counts wall-clock real time that has passed from an arbitrary
start time in the past. RDTIMEH is only present when XLEN=32 and reads
bits 63-32 of the same real-time counter.

tags.name: norm:zicntr_rdinstret_op
tags.text:
The RDINSTRET pseudoinstruction reads the low XLEN bits of the
instret CSR, which counts the number of instructions retired by this
hart from some arbitrary start point in the past. RDINSTRETH is only
present when XLEN=32 and reads bits 63-32 of the same instruction
counter.

tags.name: norm:hpmcounter_op_sz_mode
tags.text:
The Zihpm extension comprises up to 29 additional unprivileged 64-bit
hardware performance counters, hpmcounter3-hpmcounter31. When
XLEN=32, the upper 32 bits of these performance counters are accessible
via additional CSRs hpmcounter3h- hpmcounter31h.

tags.name: norm:flen_param
tags.text:
The F extension adds 32 floating-point registers, f0-f31, each 32
bits wide

tags.name: norm:D_flen_64
tags.text:
The D extension widens the 32 floating-point registers, f0-f31, to
64 bits (FLEN=64 in <<fprs>>

tags.name: norm:fld_op
tags.text:
The FLD instruction loads a double-precision floating-point value from
memory into floating-point register rd

tags.name: norm:fsd_op
tags.text:
FSD stores a double-precision
value from the floating-point registers to memory

tags.name: norm:fld_fsd_atomic_align
tags.text: FLD and FSD are only guaranteed to execute atomically if the effective address is naturally aligned and XLEN≥64.

tags.name: norm:fld_fsd_atomic_align
tags.text: FLD and FSD are only guaranteed to execute atomically if the effective address is naturally aligned and XLEN≥64.

tags.name: norm:fld_fsd_bits_maintained
tags.text: FLD and FSD do not modify the bits being transferred; in particular, the payloads of non-canonical NaNs are preserved.

tags.name: norm:fld_fsd_bits_maintained
tags.text: FLD and FSD do not modify the bits being transferred; in particular, the payloads of non-canonical NaNs are preserved.

tags.name: norm:fadd-s_fmul-s_op
tags.text:
FADD.S and FMUL.S
perform single-precision floating-point addition and multiplication
respectively, between rs1 and rs2

tags.name: norm:D_computational_instrs
tags.text: The double-precision floating-point computational instructions are defined analogously to their single-precision counterparts, but operate on double-precision operands and produce double-precision results.

tags.name: norm:FP_nontransfer_instrs_improper_nan-boxed_input
tags.text: Apart from transfer operations described in the previous paragraph, all other floating-point operations on narrower n-bit operations, n<FLEN, check if the input operands are correctly NaN-boxed, i.e., all upper FLEN-n bits are 1. If so, the n least-significant bits of the input are used as the input value, otherwise the input value is treated as an n-bit canonical NaN.

tags.name: norm:ieee_std_subnormal
tags.text: Operations on subnormal numbers are handled in accordance with the IEEE 754-2008 standard.

tags.name: norm:ieee_std_tininess
tags.text: In the parlance of the IEEE standard, tininess is detected after rounding.

tags.name: norm:dyn_round_enc
tags.text:
Rounding Mode|Mnemonic|Meaning
===
000|RNE|Round to Nearest, ties to Even
001|RTZ|Round towards Zero
010|RDN|Round Down (towards )
011|RUP|Round Up (towards )
100|RMM|Round to Nearest, ties to Max Magnitude
101||Reserved for future use.
110||Reserved for future use.
111|DYN|In instruction’s rm field, selects dynamic rounding mode; In Rounding Mode register, reserved.
===

tags.name: norm:roundingmode_dynamic
tags.text:
Floating-point operations use either a static rounding mode encoded in
the instruction, or a dynamic rounding mode held in frm. Rounding
modes are encoded as shown in <<norm:dyn_round_enc>>. A value of 111 in the
instruction's rm field selects the dynamic rounding mode held in
frm

tags.name: norm:fcvt-w-d_fcvt-l-d_op
tags.text:
FCVT.W.D or
FCVT.L.D converts a double-precision floating-point number in

tags.name: norm:fcvt-s-d_fcvt-d-s_op
tags.text:
The double-precision to single-precision and single-precision to
double-precision conversion instructions, FCVT.S.D and FCVT.D.S, are
encoded in the OP-FP major opcode space and both the source and
destination are floating-point registers. The rs2 field encodes the
datatype of the source, and the fmt field encodes the datatype of the
destination

tags.name: norm:fcvt_float_rounding
tags.text:
FCVT.S.D rounds according to the RM field; FCVT.D.S will
never round

tags.name: norm:fcvt-s-d_fcvt-d-s_op
tags.text:
The double-precision to single-precision and single-precision to
double-precision conversion instructions, FCVT.S.D and FCVT.D.S, are
encoded in the OP-FP major opcode space and both the source and
destination are floating-point registers. The rs2 field encodes the
datatype of the source, and the fmt field encodes the datatype of the
destination

tags.name: norm:fcvt_float_rounding
tags.text:
FCVT.S.D rounds according to the RM field; FCVT.D.S will
never round

tags.name: norm:fsgnj-s_fsgnjn-s_fsgnjx-s_op
tags.text:
Floating-point to floating-point sign-injection instructions, FSGNJ.S,
FSGNJN.S, and FSGNJX.S, produce a result that takes all bits except the
sign bit from rs1. For FSGNJ, the result's sign bit is rs2's sign
bit; for FSGNJN, the result's sign bit is the opposite of rs2's sign
bit; and for FSGNJX, the sign bit is the XOR of the sign bits of rs1
and rs2. Sign-injection instructions do not set floating-point
exception flags, nor do they canonicalize NaNs

tags.name: norm:fsgnj-d_fsgnjn-d_fsgnjx-d_op
tags.text: Floating-point to floating-point sign-injection instructions, FSGNJ.D, FSGNJN.D, and FSGNJX.D are defined analogously to the single-precision sign-injection instruction.

tags.name: norm:fsgnj-s_fsgnjn-s_fsgnjx-s_op
tags.text:
Floating-point to floating-point sign-injection instructions, FSGNJ.S,

bit; for FSGNJN, the result's sign bit is the opposite of rs2's sign

and rs2. Sign-injection instructions do not set floating-point

tags.name: norm:fmv-x-d_op
tags.text:
FMV.X.D moves
the double-precision value in floating-point register rs1 to a
representation in IEEE 754-2008 standard encoding in integer register
rd

tags.name: norm:fmv-d-x_op
tags.text:
FMV.D.X moves the double-precision value encoded in IEEE 754-2008
standard encoding from the integer register rs1 to the floating-point
register rd

tags.name: norm:fmv_bits_preserved
tags.text: FMV.X.D and FMV.D.X do not modify the bits being transferred; in particular, the payloads of non-canonical NaNs are preserved.

tags.name: norm:fmv_bits_preserved
tags.text: FMV.X.D and FMV.D.X do not modify the bits being transferred; in particular, the payloads of non-canonical NaNs are preserved.

tags.name: norm:feq-s_flt-s_fle-s_op
tags.text: Floating-point compare instructions (FEQ.S, FLT.S, FLE.S) perform the specified comparison between floating-point registers (, , ) writing 1 to the integer register rd if the condition holds, and 0 otherwise.

tags.name: norm:D_compare_instrs
tags.text: The double-precision floating-point compare instructions are defined analogously to their single-precision counterparts, but operate on double-precision operands.

tags.name: norm:feq-s_flt-s_fle-s_op
tags.text: Floating-point compare instructions (FEQ.S, FLT.S, FLE.S) perform the specified comparison between floating-point registers (, , ) writing 1 to the integer register rd if the condition holds, and 0 otherwise.

tags.name: norm:D_compare_instrs
tags.text: The double-precision floating-point compare instructions are defined analogously to their single-precision counterparts, but operate on double-precision operands.

tags.name: norm:feq-s_flt-s_fle-s_op
tags.text: Floating-point compare instructions (FEQ.S, FLT.S, FLE.S) perform the specified comparison between floating-point registers (, , ) writing 1 to the integer register rd if the condition holds, and 0 otherwise.

tags.name: norm:D_compare_instrs
tags.text: The double-precision floating-point compare instructions are defined analogously to their single-precision counterparts, but operate on double-precision operands.

tags.name: norm:feq-s_flt-s_fle-s_NaN_input
tags.text: For all three instructions, the result is 0 if either operand is NaN

tags.name: norm:feq-s_quiet
tags.text:
FEQ.S performs a quiet
comparison: it only sets the invalid operation exception flag if either input is a signaling NaN

tags.name: norm:D_compare_instrs
tags.text: The double-precision floating-point compare instructions are defined analogously to their single-precision counterparts, but operate on double-precision operands.

tags.name: norm:feq-s_flt-s_fle-s_NaN_input
tags.text: For all three instructions, the result is 0 if either operand is NaN

tags.name: norm:flt-s_fle-s_signaling
tags.text:
FLT.S and FLE.S perform what the IEEE 754-2008 standard refers to as
signaling comparisons: that is, they set the invalid operation
exception flag if either input is NaN

tags.name: norm:D_compare_instrs
tags.text: The double-precision floating-point compare instructions are defined analogously to their single-precision counterparts, but operate on double-precision operands.

tags.name: norm:D_compare_instrs

tags.name: norm:fclass-s_op
tags.text: fThe FCLASS.S instruction examines the value in floating-point register rs1 and writes to integer register rd a 10-bit mask that indicates the class of the floating-point number. The format of the mask is described in . The corresponding bit in rd will be set if the property is true and clear otherwise. All other bits in rd are cleared. Note that exactly one bit in rd will be set. FCLASS.S does not set the floating-point exception flags.

tags.name: norm:fclass-d_op
tags.text: The double-precision floating-point classify instruction, FCLASS.D, is defined analogously to its single-precision counterpart, but operates on double-precision operands.

tags.name: norm:fp_nan-boxing
tags.text: When multiple floating-point precisions are supported, then valid values of narrower n-bit types, n<FLEN, are represented in the lower n bits of an FLEN-bit NaN value, in a process termed NaN-boxing. The upper bits of a valid NaN-boxed value must be all 1s. Valid NaN-boxed n-bit values therefore appear as negative quiet NaNs (qNaNs) when viewed as any wider m-bit value, n < m ≤ FLEN. Any operation that writes a narrower result to an 'f' register must write all 1s to the uppermost FLEN-n bits to yield a legal NaN-boxedvalue.

tags.name: norm:FP_transfer_instrs_narrow_transfer_in
tags.text: A narrower n-bit transfer, n<FLEN, into the f registers will create a valid NaN-boxed value

tags.name: norm:FP_transfer_instrs_narrow_transfer_out
tags.text:
A narrower
n-bit transfer out of the floating-point registers will
transfer the lower n bits of the register ignoring the
upper FLEN-n bits

tags.name: norm:fcvt-w-d_fcvt-wu-d_sign_ext
tags.text:
For RV64, FCVT.W[U].D sign-extends the
32-bit result

tags.name: norm:flen_param
tags.text:
The F extension adds 32 floating-point registers, f0-f31, each 32
bits wide

tags.name: norm:fcsr_op_sz_acc
tags.text: The floating-point control and status register, fcsr, is a RISC-V control and status register (CSR). It is a 32-bit read/write register that selects the dynamic rounding mode for floating-point arithmetic operations and holds the accrued exception flags, as shown in .

tags.name: norm:fcsr_op_sz_acc
tags.text: The floating-point control and status register, fcsr, is a RISC-V control and status register (CSR). It is a 32-bit read/write register that selects the dynamic rounding mode for floating-point arithmetic operations and holds the accrued exception flags, as shown in .

tags.name: norm:fcsr_op_sz_acc
tags.text: The floating-point control and status register, fcsr, is a RISC-V control and status register (CSR). It is a 32-bit read/write register that selects the dynamic rounding mode for floating-point arithmetic operations and holds the accrued exception flags, as shown in .

tags.name: norm:fcsr_rsv
tags.text: Bits 31—​8 of the fcsr are reserved for other standard extensions. If these extensions are not present, implementations shall ignore writes to these bits and supply a zero value when read. Standard software should preserve the contents of these bits.

tags.name: norm:fflags_frm_op_sz_acc
tags.text: The fields within the fcsr can also be accessed individually through different CSR addresses, and separate assembler pseudoinstructions are defined for these accesses. The FRRM instruction reads the Rounding Mode field frm (fcsr bits 7—​5) and copies it into the least-significant three bits of integer register rd, with zero in all other bits. FSRM swaps the value in frm by copying the original value into integer register rd, and then writing a new value obtained from the three least-significant bits of integer register rs1 into frm. FRFLAGS and FSFLAGS are defined analogously for the Accrued Exception Flags field fflags (fcsr bits 4—​0).

tags.name: norm:roundingmode_dynamic
tags.text:
Floating-point operations use either a static rounding mode encoded in
the instruction, or a dynamic rounding mode held in frm. Rounding
modes are encoded as shown in <<norm:dyn_round_enc>>. A value of 111 in the
instruction's rm field selects the dynamic rounding mode held in
frm

tags.name: norm:fcsr-fflags_op
norm:roundingmode_rsv
tags.text:
The behavior of floating-point instructions that depend on
rounding mode when executed with a reserved rounding mode is reserved, including both static reserved rounding modes (101-110) and dynamic reserved rounding modes (101-111). Some instructions, including widening conversions, have the rm field but are nevertheless mathematically unaffected by the rounding mode; software should set their rm field to
RNE (000) but implementations must treat the rm field as usual (in
particular, with regard to decoding legal vs. reserved encodings).

tags.name: norm:fp_misaligned
tags.text: As described in , the execution environment defines whether misaligned floating-point loads and stores are handled invisibly or raise a contained or fatal trap.

tags.name: norm:F_rm_field
tags.text: All floating-point operations that perform rounding can select the rounding mode using the rm field with the encoding shown in .

tags.name: norm:fsw_flw_op
tags.text: Floating-point loads and stores use the same base+offset addressing mode as the integer base ISAs, with a base address in register rs1 and a 12-bit signed byte offset. The FLW instruction loads a single-precision floating-point value from memory into floating-point register rd. FSW stores a single-precision value from floating-point register rs2 to memory.

tags.name: norm:fsw_flw_op
tags.text: Floating-point loads and stores use the same base+offset addressing mode as the integer base ISAs, with a base address in register rs1 and a 12-bit signed byte offset. The FLW instruction loads a single-precision floating-point value from memory into floating-point register rd. FSW stores a single-precision value from floating-point register rs2 to memory.

tags.name: norm:fsw_flw_atomic_align
tags.text: FLW and FSW are only guaranteed to execute atomically if the effective address is naturally aligned.

tags.name: norm:fsw_flw_atomic_align

tags.name: norm:fsw_flw_bits_maintained
tags.text: FLW and FSW do not modify the bits being transferred; in particular, the payloads of non-canonical NaNs are preserved.

tags.name: norm:F_canonical_NaN
tags.text: Except when otherwise stated, if the result of a floating-point operation is NaN, it is the canonical NaN. The canonical NaN has a positive sign and all significand bits clear except the MSB, a.k.a. the quiet bit. For single-precision floating-point, this corresponds to the pattern 0x7fc00000.

tags.name: norm:fcvt_round
tags.text: All floating-point to integer and integer to floating-point conversion instructions round according to the rm field. A floating-point register can be initialized to floating-point positive zero using FCVT.S.W rd, x0, which will never set any exception flags.

for FCVT.int.S and the behavior for invalid inputs

tags.name: norm:fcvt_nx
tags.text: All floating-point conversion instructions set the Inexact exception flag if the rounded result differs from the operand value and the Invalid exception flag is not set.

destination format, it is clipped to the nearest value and the invalid
flag is set

tags.name: norm:fsgnj-s_fsgnjn-s_fsgnjx-s_op
tags.text:
Floating-point to floating-point sign-injection instructions, FSGNJ.S,
FSGNJN.S, and FSGNJX.S, produce a result that takes all bits except the
sign bit from rs1. For FSGNJ, the result's sign bit is rs2's sign
bit; for FSGNJN, the result's sign bit is the opposite of rs2's sign
bit; and for FSGNJX, the sign bit is the XOR of the sign bits of rs1
and rs2. Sign-injection instructions do not set floating-point
exception flags, nor do they canonicalize NaNs

tags.name: norm:F_fmt_single
tags.text: The 2-bit floating-point format field fmt is encoded as shown in . It is set to S (00) for all instructions in the F extension.

tags.name: norm:fsgnj-s_fsgnjn-s_fsgnjx-s_op
